principles.principle: Reuse existing styles, tokens, and components before creating anything new
The smallest change that satisfies the request is always preferred
Avoid duplicated rules across files; maintain single source of truth
Ask a clarifying question only when critical ambiguity remains
Maintain strict visual consistency across all pages and components

decision.check: Does src/tailwind.css already define the needed color?
decision.yes: Use the existing CSS variable via Tailwind token
decision.no: Only then add a new CSS variable to :root or [data-theme="light"]

decision.check: Does Tailwind spacing scale (var(--size-*)) cover the need?
decision.no: Only then add a new spacing token to theme.json

decision.check: Can existing components be composed to achieve the result?
decision.no: Check if only Tailwind class combinations are needed Use className prop extension on existing components Create a new component (must be reused in 2+ locations)

decision.check: Does src/lib/animations.ts have a matching constant?
decision.no: Create a new constant following the established pattern (duration, ease, keyframes)

consistency_rules.rule: All headings must use font-heading with established size scale
All interactive elements must have focus-visible states using --ring token
All glass panels must use the .glass-panel class, never custom CSS
All golden glow effects must use .golden-glow class or noGlow prop
All animations must use constants from src/lib/animations.ts
All components must work in both dark and light modes
No hardcoded OKLCH values in component code
BEM naming for CSS modifiers: component--variant

criteria.criterion: The pattern will be reused across 2+ locations
No existing component can achieve the result via props/composition
The pattern has distinct behavior requiring its own API

anti_criteria.anti_criterion: Only used in one location (use inline classes instead)
Different styling of an existing component (use className prop)
One-off page-specific layout (use section pattern composition)

validation_requirements.requirement: After any UI change, verify visual consistency with existing pages
Test both dark and light mode rendering
Check responsive behavior at mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints
Scan for hardcoded values that should use tokens
Ensure no visual regression in unaffected components

boundaries.rule: This mode handles UI design, component creation, styling, and visual consistency
Do NOT handle backend logic, API integration, state management, or business logic
Hand off non-UI concerns to appropriate modes (e.g., jest-test-engineer for testing)
